prevalence.trend: Increasing due to rising autoimmune cases in urban areas

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: TSH >4.5 mIU/L with low free T4; subclinical: TSH >4.5 mIU/L with normal T4 (American Thyroid Association guidelines)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: TSH-based screening adapted to Indian reference ranges (e.g., higher TSH cutoffs in some studies)
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifelong levothyroxine replacement; dose 1.6 mcg/kg body weight, monitored every 6-12 months
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Persistent iodine deficiency in sub-Himalayan and tribal areas despite iodized salt; low awareness and rural access issues; rising autoimmune cases in urban iodine-replete areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National Iodine Deficiency Disorders Control Programme (NIDDCP) has reduced goiter prevalence; affordable generic levothyroxine available
